data.title: Pirate's Golden Piles 🏴‍☠️💰
data.statement:
Ahoy, sailor! 🏴‍☠️

Captain <<Blackbeard>> and Pirate Queen <<Anne>> have discovered a legendary treasure consisting of <<several piles>> of shining gold <<coins>> 💰.

They agree to settle who gets the entire treasure with a simple but clever game:

•  The pirates take turns playing, with <<Captain Blackbeard>> going first.
•  On each turn, a player must remove <<at least one coin>> from <<exactly one pile>>.
•  The player who takes the <<last coin>> wins all the treasure! 🏆

Both pirates play <<perfectly>> with no mistakes.

Your mission, as the ship's wise parrot, is to determine <<who will win the game>>.
data.stubGenerator:
read n:int
loopline n a:long
write answer
data.inputDescription:
<<First line>>: An integer <<N>> - the number of piles

<<Second line>>: <<N>> integers <<A₁  A₂  A₃  …, Aₙ>> - the number of coins in each pile
data.outputDescription:
Print exactly <<one word >>on a single line:

[[BLACKBEARD]] if Captain Blackbeard wins the game
[[ANNE]] if Pirate Queen Anne wins the game

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

Ahoy, sailor! 🏴‍☠️

Captain Blackbeard and Pirate Queen Anne have discovered a legendary treasure consisting of several piles of shining gold coins 💰.

They agree to settle who gets the entire treasure with a simple but clever game:

• The pirates take turns playing, with Captain Blackbeard going first.
• On each turn, a player must remove at least one coin from exactly one pile.
• The player who takes the last coin wins all the treasure! 🏆

Both pirates play perfectly with no mistakes.

Your mission, as the ship's wise parrot, is to determine who will win the game.
Input
First line: An integer N - the number of piles

Second line: N integers A₁ A₂ A₃ …, Aₙ - the number of coins in each pile
Output
Print exactly one word on a single line:

BLACKBEARD if Captain Blackbeard wins the game
ANNE if Pirate Queen Anne wins the game
Constraints
1 ≤ N ≤ 100
0 ≤ Aᵢ ≤ 1 000 000 000
Example
Input
1
1
Output
BLACKBEARD
